tags.name: description
tags.text: initialize the plugin with the required options

methods.name: login
methods.signature: <T extends "apple" | "google" | "facebook" | "twitter" | "oauth2">(options: Extract<LoginOptions, { provider: T; }>) => Promise<{ provider: T; result: ProviderResponseMap[T]; }>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: Extract<{ provider: 'facebook'; options: FacebookLoginOptions; }, { provider: T; }> | Extract<{ provider: 'google'; options: GoogleLoginOptions; }, { provider: T; }> | Extract<{ provider: 'apple'; options: AppleProviderOptions; }, { provider: T; }> | Extract<{ provider: 'twitter'; options: TwitterLoginOptions; }, { provider: T; }> | Extract<{ provider: 'oauth2'; options: OAuth2LoginOptions; }, { provider: T; }>

tags.name: description
tags.text:
login with the selected provider

On user dismissal/cancellation, the Promise is rejected with `code === 'USER_CANCELLED'` (see `SocialLoginError`).

tags.name: description
tags.text:
Logout the user from the specified provider

**Google Offline Mode Limitation:**
This method is NOT supported when Google is initialized with `mode: 'offline'`.
It will reject with error: "logout is not implemented when using offline mode"

tags.name: throws
tags.text: Error if Google provider is in offline mode

tags.name: description
tags.text:
Check if the user is currently logged in with the specified provider

**Google Offline Mode Limitation:**
This method is NOT supported when Google is initialized with `mode: 'offline'`.
It will reject with error: "isLoggedIn is not implemented when using offline mode"

tags.name: throws
tags.text: Error if Google provider is in offline mode

tags.name: description
tags.text:
Get the authorization code for server-side authentication

**Google Offline Mode Limitation:**
This method is NOT supported when Google is initialized with `mode: 'offline'`.
It will reject with error: "getAuthorizationCode is not implemented when using offline mode"

In offline mode, the authorization code (serverAuthCode) is already returned by the `login()` method.

tags.name: throws
tags.text: Error if Google provider is in offline mode

tags.name: description
tags.text:
refresh the access token

**Google Offline Mode Limitation:**
This method is NOT supported when Google is initialized with `mode: 'offline'`.
Offline mode only returns `serverAuthCode` for backend token exchange, so token refresh must happen on your backend.
The plugin logs and rejects with a message explaining that you should send `serverAuthCode` to your backend,
refresh the Google tokens there, or switch to `mode: 'online'` for client-side refresh.

**Google Web Limitation:**
On Web, Google `refresh()` is not implemented, even when using `mode: 'online'`.
Call `login()` again on Web to obtain a fresh token instead.

tags.name: throws
tags.text: Error if Google provider is in offline mode, or on Web where Google `refresh()` is not implemented

methods.name: refreshToken
methods.signature: (options: { provider: 'oauth2'; providerId: string; refreshToken?: string; additionalParameters?: Record<string, string>; }) => Promise<OAuth2LoginResponse>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { provider: 'oauth2'; providerId: string; refreshToken?: string | undefined; additionalParameters?: Record<string, string> | undefined; }

methods.docs:
OAuth2 refresh-token helper (feature parity with Capawesome OAuth).

Scope:
- Only applies to the built-in `oauth2` provider (not Google/Apple/Facebook/Twitter).
- Requires a token endpoint (either `accessTokenEndpoint`/`tokenEndpoint` or `issuerUrl` discovery).

Security note:
- This does not validate JWT signatures. It only exchanges/refreshes tokens.

If `refreshToken` is omitted, the plugin will attempt to use the stored refresh token (if available).

methods.name: handleRedirectCallback
methods.docs:
Web-only: handle the OAuth redirect callback and return the parsed result.

Notes:
- This is only meaningful on Web. iOS/Android implementations will reject.
- Intended for redirect-based flows (e.g. `oauth2` with `flow: 'redirect'`) where the page navigates away.

methods.name: decodeIdToken
methods.signature: (options: { idToken?: string; token?: string; }) => Promise<{ claims: Record<string, any>; }>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { idToken?: string | undefined; token?: string | undefined; }

methods.docs:
Decode a JWT (typically an OIDC ID token) into its claims.

Notes:
- Accepts both `idToken` and `token` to match common naming (Capawesome uses `token`).
- This does not validate the signature or issuer/audience. It only base64url-decodes the payload.

methods.name: getAccessTokenExpirationDate
methods.docs:
Convert an access token expiration timestamp (milliseconds since epoch) to an ISO date string.

This is a pure helper (feature parity with Capawesome OAuth) and does not depend on provider state.

methods.name: isAccessTokenAvailable
methods.signature: (options: { accessToken: string | null; }) => Promise<{ isAvailable: boolean; }>
methods.docs:
Check if an access token is available (non-empty).

This is a pure helper (feature parity with Capawesome OAuth) and does not depend on provider state.

methods.name: isAccessTokenExpired
methods.docs:
Check if an access token is expired.

This is a pure helper (feature parity with Capawesome OAuth) and does not depend on provider state.

methods.name: isRefreshTokenAvailable
methods.signature: (options: { refreshToken: string | null; }) => Promise<{ isAvailable: boolean; }>
methods.docs:
Check if a refresh token is available (non-empty).

This is a pure helper (feature parity with Capawesome OAuth) and does not depend on provider state.

methods.name: openSecureWindow

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - the options for the openSecureWindow call

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - the options for the openSecureWindow call

methods.docs:
Opens a secured window for OAuth2 authentication.
For web, you should have the code in the redirected page to use a broadcast channel to send the redirected url to the app
Something like:
```html
<html>
<head></head>
<body>
<script>
  const searchParams = new URLSearchParams(location.search)
  if (searchParams.has("code")) {
    new BroadcastChannel("my-channel-name").postMessage(location.href);
    window.close();
  }
</script>
</body>
</html>
```
For mobile, you should have a redirect uri that opens the app, something like: `myapp://oauth_callback/`
And make sure to register it in the app's info.plist:
```xml
<key>CFBundleURLTypes</key>
<array>
   <dict>
      <key>CFBundleURLSchemes</key>
      <array>
         <string>myapp</string>
      </array>
   </dict>
</array>
```
And in the AndroidManifest.xml file:
```xml
<activity>
   <intent-filter>
      <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
      <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
      <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
      <data android:host="oauth_callback" android:scheme="myapp" />
   </intent-filter>
</activity>
```

properties.name: oauth2

tags.name: example

properties.docs:
OAuth2 provider configurations.
Supports multiple providers by using a Record with provider IDs as keys.

properties.name: twitter
properties.type: { clientId: string; redirectUrl: string; defaultScopes?: string[] | undefined; forceLogin?: boolean | undefined; audience?: string | undefined; } | undefined

properties.name: facebook
properties.type: { appId: string; clientToken?: string | undefined; locale?: string | undefined; } | undefined

properties.name: google
properties.type: { iOSClientId?: string | undefined; iOSServerClientId?: string | undefined; webClientId?: string | undefined; mode?: 'online' | 'offline' | undefined; hostedDomain?: string | undefined; redirectUrl?: string | undefined; } | undefined

properties.name: apple
properties.type: { clientId?: string | undefined; redirectUrl?: string | undefined; useProperTokenExchange?: boolean | undefined; useBroadcastChannel?: boolean | undefined; } | undefined

interfaces.name: OAuth2ProviderConfig
interfaces.docs: Configuration for a single OAuth2 provider instance

properties.name: appId
properties.docs:
The OAuth 2.0 client identifier (App ID / Client ID).

Note: this configuration object is only used by the plugin's built-in `oauth2` provider
(i.e. `SocialLogin.initialize({ oauth2: { ... } })`). It does not affect Google/Apple/Facebook/Twitter.

properties.name: clientId
properties.docs:
Alias for `appId` to match common OAuth/OIDC naming (`clientId`).
If both are provided, `appId` takes precedence.

properties.name: issuerUrl
properties.docs:
OpenID Connect issuer URL (enables discovery via `/.well-known/openid-configuration`).
When set, you may omit explicit endpoints like `authorizationBaseUrl` and `accessTokenEndpoint`.

Notes:
- Explicit endpoints (authorization/token/logout) take precedence over discovered values.
- Discovery is supported for `oauth2` on Web, iOS, and Android.

properties.name: authorizationBaseUrl
properties.docs: The base URL of the authorization endpoint

properties.name: authorizationEndpoint
properties.docs: Alias for `authorizationBaseUrl` (to match common OAuth/OIDC naming).

properties.name: clientSecret
properties.docs:
OAuth 2.0 client secret for token requests (e.g., when exchanging the code).
This value is sent as `client_secret` in token/refresh requests when provided.

properties.name: accessTokenEndpoint
properties.docs:
The URL to exchange the authorization code for tokens
Required for authorization code flow

properties.name: tokenEndpoint
properties.docs: Alias for `accessTokenEndpoint` (to match common OAuth/OIDC naming).

properties.name: redirectUrl
properties.docs: Redirect URL that receives the OAuth callback

properties.name: resourceUrl
properties.docs:
Optional URL to fetch user profile/resource data after authentication
The access token will be sent as Bearer token in the Authorization header

properties.name: responseType
properties.docs:
The OAuth response type
- 'code': Authorization Code flow (recommended, requires accessTokenEndpoint)
- 'token': Implicit flow (less secure, tokens returned directly)

properties.name: pkceEnabled
properties.docs:
Enable PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange)
Strongly recommended for public clients (mobile/web apps)

properties.name: scope
properties.docs: Default scopes to request during authorization

properties.name: scopes
properties.docs:
Alias for `scope` using common naming (`scopes`).
If both are provided, `scope` takes precedence.

properties.name: additionalParameters
properties.docs: Additional parameters to include in the authorization request

properties.name: loginHint
properties.docs:
Convenience option for OIDC `login_hint`.
Equivalent to passing `additionalParameters.login_hint`.

properties.name: prompt
properties.docs:
Convenience option for OAuth/OIDC `prompt`.
Equivalent to passing `additionalParameters.prompt`.

properties.name: additionalTokenParameters
properties.docs:
Additional parameters to include in token requests (code exchange / refresh).
Useful for providers that require non-standard parameters.

properties.name: additionalResourceHeaders
properties.docs: Additional headers to include when fetching the resource URL

properties.name: logoutUrl
properties.docs: Custom logout URL for ending the session

properties.name: endSessionEndpoint
properties.docs: Alias for `logoutUrl` to match OIDC naming (`endSessionEndpoint`).

properties.name: postLogoutRedirectUrl
properties.docs: OIDC post logout redirect URL (sent as `post_logout_redirect_uri` when building the end-session URL).

properties.name: additionalLogoutParameters
properties.docs: Additional parameters to include in logout / end-session URL.

properties.name: iosPrefersEphemeralWebBrowserSession
properties.docs:
iOS-only: Whether to prefer an ephemeral browser session for ASWebAuthenticationSession.
Defaults to true to match existing behavior in this plugin.

properties.name: iosPrefersEphemeralSession
properties.docs: Alias for `iosPrefersEphemeralWebBrowserSession` (to match Capawesome OAuth naming).

properties.name: logsEnabled
properties.docs: Enable debug logging

properties.name: profile
properties.type: { userID: string; email: string | null; friendIDs: string[]; birthday: string | null; ageRange: { min?: number | undefined; max?: number | undefined; } | null; gender: string | null; location: { id: string; name: string; } | null; hometown: { id: string; name: string; } | null; profileURL: string | null; name: string | null; imageURL: string | null; }

properties.name: profile
properties.type: { email: string | null; familyName: string | null; givenName: string | null; id: string | null; name: string | null; imageUrl: string | null; }

properties.name: accessToken

tags.text:
Content depends on `useProperTokenExchange` setting:
- When `useProperTokenExchange: true`: Real access token from Apple (~1 hour validity)
- When `useProperTokenExchange: false`: Contains authorization code as token (legacy mode)
Use `idToken` for user authentication, `accessToken` for API calls when properly exchanged.
tags.name: description

properties.docs: Access token from Apple

properties.name: idToken

tags.text:
Always contains the JWT with user identity information including:
- User ID (sub claim)
- Email (if user granted permission)
- Name components (if user granted permission)
- Email verification status
This is the primary token for user authentication and should be verified on your backend.
tags.name: description

properties.docs: Identity token (JWT) from Apple

properties.name: profile

tags.text:
Basic user profile data extracted from the identity token and Apple response:
- `user`: Apple's user identifier (sub claim from idToken)
- `email`: User's email address (if permission granted)
- `givenName`: User's first name (if permission granted)
- `familyName`: User's last name (if permission granted)
tags.name: description

properties.docs: User profile information
properties.type: { user: string; email: string | null; givenName: string | null; familyName: string | null; }

properties.name: authorizationCode

tags.text:
Only present when `useProperTokenExchange` is `true`. This code should be exchanged
for proper access tokens on your backend using Apple's token endpoint. Use this for secure
server-side token validation and to obtain refresh tokens.
tags.name: description

properties.docs: Authorization code for proper token exchange (when useProperTokenExchange is enabled)

properties.name: providerId
properties.docs: The provider ID that was used for this login

properties.name: accessToken
properties.docs: The access token received from the OAuth provider

properties.name: idToken
properties.docs: The ID token (JWT) if provided by the OAuth server (e.g., OpenID Connect)

properties.name: refreshToken
properties.docs: The refresh token if provided (requires appropriate scope like offline_access)

properties.name: resourceData
properties.docs:
Resource data fetched from resourceUrl if configured
Contains the raw JSON response from the resource endpoint

properties.name: scope
properties.docs: The scopes that were granted

properties.name: tokenType
properties.docs: Token type (usually 'bearer')

properties.name: expiresIn
properties.docs: Token expiration time in seconds

properties.name: permissions

tags.text:
Select permissions to login with. Supports both consumer and business permissions.

**Consumer Permissions:**
- `email` - User's email address
- `public_profile` - User's public profile info
- `user_friends` - List of friends who also use your app

**Business Permissions** (require business app configuration and may need App Review):
- `instagram_basic` - Instagram Basic Display API access
- `instagram_manage_insights` - Instagram Insights data
- `instagram_manage_comments` - Manage Instagram comments
- `instagram_content_publish` - Publish to Instagram
- `pages_show_list` - List of Pages managed by user
- `pages_read_engagement` - Read Page engagement metrics
- `pages_manage_posts` - Manage Page posts
- `pages_messaging` - Page messaging features
- `business_management` - Manage business assets
- `catalog_management` - Manage product catalogs
- `ads_management` - Manage advertising accounts
tags.name: description

tags.text: docs /facebook_business_login.md for complete business integration guide
tags.name: see

properties.docs: Permissions

properties.name: limitedLogin

tags.text:
use limited login for Facebook iOS only. Important: This is iOS-only and doesn't affect Android.
Even if set to false, Facebook will automatically force it to true if App Tracking Transparency (ATT) permission is not granted.
Developers should always be prepared to handle both limited and full login scenarios.
tags.name: description

properties.name: filterByAuthorizedAccounts

tags.text:
Only show accounts that have previously been used to sign in to the app.
This option is only available for the 'bottom' style.
Note: For Family Link supervised accounts, this should be set to false.
tags.name: description

properties.docs: Filter by authorized accounts (Android only)

properties.name: autoSelectEnabled

tags.text:
Automatically select the account if only one Google account is available.
This option is only available for the 'bottom' style.
tags.name: description

properties.docs: Auto select enabled (Android only)

properties.name: prompt

tags.text:
A space-delimited, case-sensitive list of prompts to present the user.
If you don't specify this parameter, the user will be prompted only the first time your project requests access.

**Possible values:**
- `none`: Don't display any authentication or consent screens. Must not be specified with other values.
- `consent`: Prompt the user for consent.
- `select_account`: Prompt the user to select an account.

**Examples:**
- `prompt: 'consent'` - Always show consent screen
- `prompt: 'select_account'` - Always show account selection
- `prompt: 'consent select_account'` - Show both consent and account selection

**Note:** This parameter only affects web platform behavior. Mobile platforms use their own native prompts.
tags.name: description

properties.docs: Prompt parameter for Google OAuth (Web only)

tags.text: An array of scopes to request during login

When enabled, uses Broadcast Channel API for communication instead of URL redirects.
Only applicable on platforms that support Broadcast Channel (Android).

properties.name: scopes
properties.docs:
Additional scopes to request during login.
If omitted the plugin falls back to the default scopes configured during initialization.

properties.name: state
properties.docs:
Provide a custom OAuth state value.
When not provided the plugin generates a cryptographically random value.

properties.name: codeVerifier
properties.docs:
Provide a pre-computed PKCE code verifier (mostly used for testing).
When omitted the plugin generates a secure verifier automatically.

properties.name: redirectUrl
properties.docs:
Override the redirect URI for a single login call.
Useful when the same app supports multiple callback URLs per platform.

properties.name: forceLogin
properties.docs: Force the consent screen on every attempt, maps to `force_login=true`.

properties.name: providerId
properties.docs:
The provider ID as configured in initialize()
This is required to identify which OAuth2 provider to use

properties.name: scope
properties.docs:
Override the scopes for this login request
If not provided, uses the scopes from initialization

properties.name: scopes
properties.docs:
Alias for `scope` using common naming (`scopes`).
If both are provided, `scope` takes precedence.

properties.name: state
properties.docs:
Custom state parameter for CSRF protection
If not provided, a random value is generated

properties.name: codeVerifier
properties.docs:
Override PKCE code verifier (for testing purposes)
If not provided, a secure random verifier is generated

properties.name: redirectUrl
properties.docs: Override redirect URL for this login request

properties.name: additionalParameters
properties.docs: Additional parameters to add to the authorization URL

properties.name: loginHint
properties.docs:
Convenience option for OIDC `login_hint`.
Equivalent to passing `additionalParameters.login_hint`.

properties.name: prompt
properties.docs:
Convenience option for OAuth/OIDC `prompt`.
Equivalent to passing `additionalParameters.prompt`.

properties.name: flow
properties.docs:
Web-only (`oauth2` provider only): Use a full-page redirect instead of a popup window.

When using `redirect`, the promise returned by `login()` will not resolve because the page navigates away.
After the redirect lands back in your app, call `SocialLogin.handleRedirectCallback()` on that page to
parse the result.
